evals.name: chatgpt-controls-and-citations
evals.prompt: Extract this ChatGPT paste containing You and ChatGPT turns, Copy and Good response controls, inline citations, and a Sources drawer.

expectations.text: Response uses ChatGPT response controls as assistant-boundary evidence.
Response catalogs inline citations and the Sources drawer as source traces.
Response excludes Copy and Good response UI chrome from the semantic summary.
Response creates Turn ledger and Content element ledger sections.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the chatgpt-controls-and-citations scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.

evals.name: chatgpt-canvas-and-files
evals.prompt: The ChatGPT thread references an uploaded spreadsheet, a generated PNG, and a Canvas document exported as Markdown, but only the chat text and file names were pasted.

expectations.text: Response catalogs the spreadsheet, PNG, and Canvas document separately.
Response treats Canvas as a separate surface from the chat.
Response assigns metadata-only or referenced-not-supplied fidelity where content is absent.
Response does not claim to have inspected missing files.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the chatgpt-canvas-and-files scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.

evals.name: chatgpt-ambiguous-full-paste
evals.prompt: Normalize a control-all ChatGPT paste with flattened formatting, a quoted user prompt inside an answer, and no visible role names.

expectations.text: Response does not classify roles from prose style alone.
Response uses low confidence when relying on alternation.
Response does not automatically split a quoted prompt into a human turn.
Response records unresolved boundaries in Normalization exceptions.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the chatgpt-ambiguous-full-paste scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.
